results.possibleIdentity: Charles Kennedy was an engineer based in New York City during the late 1960s and early 1970s. He was employed by the GBC American Corporation (also known as GBC Closed Circuit TV Corp.), a specialist in Closed Circuit Television (CCTV) systems located at 89 Franklin Street, New York, NY 10013. His primary technical expertise included video tape technology and Closed Circuit Television (CCTV). Kennedy was a member of Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a service organization that facilitated collaborations between the technical and artistic communities. Within the E.A.T. network, Kennedy served as a technical consultant, providing engineers' expertise to artists working with emerging video media. During this period, GBC was a major supplier of video equipment used by pioneering video artists such as Nam June Paik and the Vasulkas.
